perturbation.original_text: Term Section 1.01. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.changed_text: Term Section 1.01. The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and will automatically renew yearly unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract states that the agreement lasts for three years. The modified version removes this clause and indicates that it will renew yearly. The contradictory element is that Article VI remains unchanged referencing the initial term, creating confusion and contradiction regarding the agreement's duration. If the original term section stipulated a 3 year term with termination clauses, the edited section stipulates an automatic renewal clause without any termination clause other than those mentioned in Article VI.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE VI [Termination] Termination Section 6.01 [Termination for Cause]. Termination for Cause. In the event that either PrimeCall or DeltaThree materially breaches any of its duties or obligations hereunder, which breach shall not be cured within thirty (30) days after written notice is given to the breaching party specifying the breach, then either PrimeCall or DeltaThree, as the case may be, may, by giving written notice thereof to the other, terminate this Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination, which 4 date shall be no earlier than ten (10) days after the date of such notice. Section 6.02 [Termination for Bankruptcy]. Termination for Bankruptcy. In the event of the Bankruptcy (as hereinafter defined) of either PrimeCall or DeltaThree, then the non-bankrupt party may, by written notice thereof to the party in Bankruptcy, terminate this Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination, which date shall be no earlier than ten (10) days after the date of such notice. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Bankruptcy" shall mean the happening of any of the following: (i) the filing of an application for, or a consent to, the appointment of a trustee for all or substantially all of the relevant party's assets, (ii) the filing of a voluntary petition in bankruptcy, or the filing of a pleading in any court of record admitting in writing the relevant party's inability to pay its debts generally as they come due, (iii) the making of a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, (iv) the entry of an order, judgment or decree by any court of competent jurisdiction adjudicating the relevant party a bankrupt, or appointing a trustee of all or substantially all of such party's assets unless such order, judgment or decree is vacated or stayed on appeal within thirty (30) days or (v) the filing of an involuntary case or other proceeding against the relevant party seeking liquidation, reorganization or other relief under any bankruptcy, insolvency or other similar law, which case or proceeding shall not have been dismissed within sixty days after filing. Section 6.03 [Effect of Termination]. Effect of Termination. In the event of the termination of this Agreement, all rights and obligations of PrimeCall and DeltaThree shall terminate as of the effective date of such termination, except that (i) such termination shall not constitute a waiver of any rights that either PrimeCall or DeltaThree may have by reason of a breach of this Agreement, (ii) such termination shall not constitute a waiver of any right to receive payments that are due and owing pursuant to this Agreement and (iii) the provisions of Article VII [Limited Warranty] shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a three-year term, whereas the changed text introduces an automatic yearly renewal. This directly contradicts Article VI, which details termination clauses applicable to the 'Agreement' without specifying whether these apply to the initial term only or also to any renewal terms. This omission creates ambiguity about the term's duration and enforceability of termination rights detailed in Article VI, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. The original text states the agreement lasts for three years. The modified version removes this clause and indicates that it will renew yearly. The contradictory element is that Article VI remains unchanged referencing the initial term, creating confusion and contradiction regarding the agreement's duration.

perturbation.original_text: PrimeCall and DeltaThree each agree that for the longest period permitted by law each shall hold in strictest confidence and, without the prior written approval of the other party hereto, not to use for their own benefit or the benefit of any party other than the other party hereto, or disclose to any person, firm or corporation other than such party (other than as required by law) any confidential proprietary information concerning the business and affairs of the other party hereto; provided, however, that the foregoing limitations and restrictions shall not apply to information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the directors, officers, shareholders, partners, affiliates, employees, agents or advisors of PrimeCall or DeltaThree, as the case may be, or (ii) is or becomes available to PrimeCall or DeltaThree on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the other party hereto or any of its advisors, agents or affiliates, provided that such source is not known by PrimeCall or DeltaThree, as the case may be, to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other obligation of secrecy to the other party hereto. Each of PrimeCall and DeltaThree recognize that the absence of a time limitation in this Section 8.01 [Confidentiality] is reasonable and properly required for the protection of the other party hereto and in the event that the absence of such limitation is deemed to be unreasonable by a court of competent jurisdiction, PrimeCall and DeltaThree each agree and submit to the imposition of such a limitation as said court shall deem reasonable.
perturbation.changed_text: PrimeCall and DeltaThree each agree to hold confidential proprietary information for a duration specified by prevailing legal standards in their respective jurisdictions. Each party shall not, without prior written approval from the other, use said information for their own benefit or disclose it to any third party. The parties recognize that the exact duration of confidentiality is subject to legal interpretation and may vary. Information is exempt if it becomes publicly available through no fault of either party, or if obtained from a third party not bound by confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement emphasizes indefinite confidentiality, only limited by what the law permits ('for the longest period permitted by law') and reinforces this with a specific acknowledgement of the reasonableness of this open-ended commitment. The altered section now introduces uncertainty, stating the confidential information should be held for a duration specified by prevailing legal standards in their respective jurisdictions, but states that the exact duration of confidentiality is subject to legal interpretation and may vary. This contradicts Section 8.02 because it weakens the protection and creates uncertainty that could undermine the basis for injunctive relief if a breach occurs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8.02 [Confidentiality]. Equitable Remedies PrimeCall and DeltaThree each specifically recognize that any breach of Section 8.01 [Confidentiality] will cause irreparable injury to the other party hereto and that actual damages may be difficult to ascertain, and in any event, may be inadequate. Accordingly 6 (and without limiting the availability of legal or equitable, including injunctive, remedies under any other provisions of this Agreement), each of PrimeCall and DeltaThree agrees that in the event of any such breach, the other party hereto shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to such other legal and equitable remedies that may be available. In addition, PrimeCall and DeltaThree each agree that the provisions of Section 8.01 [Confidentiality] shall be considered separate and apart from the remaining provisions of this Agreement and shall be enforced as such.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates confidentiality for the longest period permitted by law, indicating a strong preference for indefinite confidentiality. The changed text introduces uncertainty by limiting confidentiality to a duration specified by legal standards, which are subject to interpretation. This directly contradicts Section 8.02 [Confidentiality]. Equitable Remedies, because Section 8.02 relies on the certainty of Section 8.01 to justify injunctive relief. By weakening Section 8.01, the changed text undermines the justification for equitable remedies in Section 8.02.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by DeltaThree without the prior written consent of PrimeCall. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall or in connection with a merger or consolidation of PrimeCall or a sale of all or substantially all of PrimeCall's business. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, this Agreement may not be assigned by PrimeCall without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either DeltaThree or PrimeCall without prior written consent from both parties, except in cases of merger or acquisition. In cases of merger or acquisition, the agreement can be assigned without consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause imposes stricter conditions on DeltaThree, preventing it from assigning the agreement without PrimeCall’s consent, while granting PrimeCall more latitude to assign it to affiliates or in the event of a major business change. By amending the text to require consent from both parties or allow assignment without consent in cases of merger or acquisition introduces ambiguity with regards to the original clause, since the contract does not define the requirements for 'consent'. This undermines the third party clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9.13 [Third Parties]. Third Parties. There are no intended third party beneficiaries to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows PrimeCall to assign the agreement under specific circumstances (affiliates, merger, sale), while DeltaThree requires PrimeCall's consent. The changed text fundamentally alters this by requiring consent from *both* parties or allowing assignment without consent in cases of merger or acquisition. This directly contradicts Section 9.13, which states there are no intended third-party beneficiaries. The original agreement, by granting PrimeCall more latitude in assignment, implicitly benefits PrimeCall. By removing this, the changed text removes this benefit, thereby contradicting the intention of the original agreement and harming PrimeCall as a beneficiary. The imposition of consent where it was not required, and the introduction of merger/acquisition exceptions when previously PrimeCall had more specific assignment allowances, creates an inconsistency. This introduces a strong conflict making the agreement unclear and unenforceable, and therefore, a contradiction exists.
